notes: Synthetic Phase 4 D-01 Rust SSTI fixture inventory.
These cases are not real CVEs and must not be reported as CVE-backed validation.
Positive cases model attacker-controlled template source; negative cases bind user input only as template data or use compile-time templates.

fixtures.misuse_pattern: User input flows into Tera::one_off() template source.
fixtures.cwe_rationale: Attacker-controlled input is evaluated as server-side template syntax rather than bound only as data.

fixtures.misuse_pattern: Server-controlled template file rendered with user input as context data.
fixtures.cwe_rationale: No CWE-1336 issue is present because user input is not template source.

fixtures.misuse_pattern: User input flows into Environment::render_str() template source.
fixtures.cwe_rationale: Attacker-controlled input is evaluated as server-side template syntax rather than bound only as data.

fixtures.misuse_pattern: Server-controlled named template rendered with user input as context data.
fixtures.cwe_rationale: No CWE-1336 issue is present because user input is not template source.

fixtures.misuse_pattern: Compile-time Askama templates receive user input only as struct fields.
fixtures.cwe_rationale: No CWE-1336 issue is present because Askama does not parse attacker-controlled template source at runtime.

fixtures.misuse_pattern: User input flows into Handlebars::render_template() template source.
fixtures.cwe_rationale: Attacker-controlled input is evaluated as server-side template syntax rather than bound only as data.

fixtures.misuse_pattern: Server-controlled registered template rendered with user input as data.
fixtures.cwe_rationale: No CWE-1336 issue is present because user input is not template source.
